reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C-O and C-C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N-O bond (loss of water from nitrile oxide precursor)

feed_streams.reasoning: All reagents are premixed in DES; phenylacetylene must be present when nitrile oxide forms in situ to trap it and prevent dimerization to furoxan. No incompatibility between substrates at room temperature.

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide in situ via thermal dehydration; 2 equiv used to drive reaction to completion

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.role: deep eutectic solvent component (hydrogen bond acceptor)
reagents.notes: Forms DES with ethylene glycol; acts as reaction medium and may promote nitrile oxide formation

reagents.name: ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.role: deep eutectic solvent component (hydrogen bond donor)

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal tautomerization/dehydration of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (nitrile oxide intermediate) in situ. The nitro group undergoes aci-nitro tautomerism followed by loss of water.

Concerted [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide 1,3-dipole with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate. Regioselectivity favors 3,5-disubstituted isoxazole due to electronic and steric factors.

stream_logic.reasoning: Single premixed feed: all components are compatible at room temperature. Phenylacetylene must be present when nitrile oxide forms at elevated temperature to trap it immediately and suppress dimerization to furoxan. DES is the solvent. Concentration ~0.5 M phenylacetylene, ~1.0 M ethyl nitroacetate in 1 mL DES.

mixing_order_reasoning: All reagents are dissolved together in the DES at room temperature before entering the heated reactor. This ensures the dipolarophile (phenylacetylene) is present to trap the reactive nitrile oxide intermediate as soon as it forms at 120°C, preventing competitive dimerization. No separate streams are needed since there is no incompatibility at ambient temperature.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species or radicals involved; thermal pericyclic mechanism is unaffected by O2.
quench_reasoning: Reaction reaches full conversion in 15 min at 120°C. In flow, simply cooling the output stream to room temperature effectively stops the reaction. Batch protocol uses water quench only for workup/extraction purposes, not to terminate a dangerous reaction.
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction driven by heating to 120°C. No photocatalyst or photochemical activation is involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism: well-established nitrile oxide generation from nitroacetate and [3+2] cycloaddition. The DES (TBAB/EG 1:5) is quite viscous at room temperature (~50-100 cP) which may pose pumping challenges in narrow-bore tubing; a wider ID coil (e.g., 1.0-1.6 mm) or slight preheating of the feed may be needed. The 120°C temperature in a pressurized flow system (back-pressure regulator) should be straightforward. The 15 min residence time is well-suited for flow. Uncertainty: whether the DES can be recycled inline or if product extraction must be done offline. The 2.0 equiv nitroacetate suggests some decomposition of the nitrile oxide occurs even with dipolarophile present.
